description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a spiny crab, featuring a broad, domed carapace with a rough, spiked texture and mottled brown and cream coloration. The crab has two prominent claws (chelae) at the front, each covered in spines and ending in sharp pincers. Its body is segmented into a cephalothorax and a small, tucked abdomen, with ten legs in total, including the claws. The walking legs are long, jointed, and covered in fine bristles, with a reddish-brown patterning that contrasts with the lighter underbody. Small, stalked eyes are visible at the front corners of the carapace, and short antennae are present but not highly prominent. The overall appearance is robust and armored, typical of marine crustaceans found in coastal environments.
description: A spiny, brown-and-cream crab with a domed, rough carapace, two large claws, and long, jointed legs. Its body is segmented and covered in bristles and spines.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, spiked carapace, both large claws extended, and the eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the curvature of the carapace, the segmentation of the legs, and the bristled texture.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the carapace, the symmetrical arrangement of the legs, and the tucked abdomen.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the jointed legs, spiny surface, and the crab's overall robust form.
